review.summary: The executor is well-designed with strong fail-closed controls, but the commit under review adds a task-profile routing path (advanced/insight/design -> Fable 5) whose model IDs (claude-fable-5) are unverified and never proven reachable, and the routing tests only exercise resolve_model in isolation. A budget-bypass gap exists: reserve_attempt only enforces the repository daily cap against same-day calls, but window and duplicate-diff checks scan the entire ledger with no bound, and the elevated model path lacks a test proving --fallback-model is actually invoked end-to-end through review(). SECURITY.md itself flags public_dispatch_metadata_accepted as an open disclosure of private Season 2 Head SHA and request IDs via Actions event metadata.

findings.finding: scripts/local_claude_bridge.py routes advanced/insight/design profiles to ELEVATED_MODEL='claude-fable-5', an unverified model ID with no proof it exists or is entitled on the subscription. invoke_claude only appends --fallback-model for ELEVATED_MODEL, but no test exercises the full review() path with the elevated model; the failure mode if Fable 5 is unavailable and fallback also fails is untested. README claims Fable 5 is 'the most advanced' with Opus 4.8 fallback, an unsupported capability claim.

SECURITY.md item 8 documents that public dispatch exposes PR number, Head SHA, reservation, and request identifiers as Actions event metadata; public_dispatch_metadata_accepted defaults false and the config requires it true to activate. This is a live private-state disclosure (Season 2 Head SHA and request IDs leak to a public repo's Actions log) that the diff acknowledges but does not close; an opaque broker is only 'preferred', not implemented.

reserve_attempt duplicate-diff check scans the entire ledger (all history, unbounded) while window and daily caps only filter by day/window. A ledger that grows without pruning makes every reservation O(n) and the duplicate check permanently blocks re-review of a legitimately re-opened identical diff. No ledger rotation or retention is defined.

test_task_profiles_route_models_deterministically and test_elevated_model_can_fall_back_only_to_default assert command construction but never invoke the real 'claude' binary, so the tests prove argument wiring, not that claude-fable-5 or its fallback actually resolve. The commit message 'prove model and budget routing paths' overstates what the mock-only tests demonstrate.

No-tools isolation, secret-pattern rejection, OS file-lock serialization, duplicate-diff denial, output/ledger repository-boundary confinement, disallowed-auth-env rejection, and fail-closed unstructured-output handling are correctly implemented and covered by tests.

verification: Diff proves --tools "", --strict-mcp-config, --setting-sources "" and DISALLOWED_AUTH_ENV rejection deny file/shell/MCP and API-key overrides.
Concurrent reservation test proves the OS flock permits exactly one of eight racing identical-diff reservations.
Cap tests prove 6/window, 2 windows/work-item/day, 12/repo/day limits reject the boundary-crossing attempt.
Duplicate-key and prose fallback tests prove unstructured output forces CHANGES_REQUESTED and never authorizes approval.
validate_result rejects APPROVE with open P0/P1/P2 and strips hidden-markup/field-injection from model text.
Workflow static tests confirm dispatch-only, immutable action SHAs, read-only token, rerun denial, and minimized run-name.
requirement_coverage: Credential exposure: covered by secret-pattern scan on diff and model text plus DISALLOWED_AUTH_ENV.
Budget bypass: covered by ledger caps and flock, but unbounded duplicate-diff scan is a residual gap.
Fail-closed: covered by unstructured/duplicate-key/schema-drift forcing CHANGES_REQUESTED.
Unsafe automation: covered by no-tools, no merge/push/deploy, dispatch-only workflow.
Unsupported claims: partially covered; Fable 5 capability claim in README is unverified.
Tests proving claim: routing tests are mock-only and do not prove real model reachability.
residual_risk: claude-fable-5 model ID may not exist or be entitled; runtime failure is untested beyond mocks.
Public Actions metadata leaks Season 2 Head SHA and request IDs until an opaque broker replaces it.
Unbounded ledger growth degrades reservation performance and permanently blocks identical re-reviews.
Administrator can revert branch/environment protection; mitigated only by required user-approved SHA.
